summary: Ukraine conducted deep strikes into Russian territory targeting the VNIIR-Progress plant in Cheboksary and the Novokuibyshevsk oil refinery in Samara, over 900 km from the front line. Russia launched a large-scale drone and missile attack on Ukraine overnight, with 326 Ukrainian drones reportedly shot down over Russian regions. Russian forces made significant advances into Kostiantynovka, breaking into the city center from multiple directions.
key_points: Ukraine struck VNIIR-Progress plant in Cheboksary and Novokuibyshevsk refinery in Samara using FP-5 Flamingo missiles, over 900 km from the front. (Source: @UkraineNow/81810)
Russia launched 650 drones and 35-100 missiles per day at Ukraine; Ukraine responded with 300-350 drones daily. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802)
326 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian regions overnight; fires hit Samara refinery and a fuel container in Rostov region. (Source: @tass_agency/380029)
Russian troops broke into Kostiantynovka city center from Illinovka and Berestok, advancing through the 7 Vetrov shopping center and Yubileiny Park. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30804)
Ukraine destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure including power substations, radar equipment, fuel tanks, and the shadow fleet vessel Lady Augusta. (Source: @UkraineNow/81817)
EU Commission proposed banning entry to all Russian military personnel involved in the war and announced a 21st sanctions package targeting energy and fishing. (Source: @UkraineNow/81798)
Bulgaria announced it will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine, stating Kiev needs more people, not more weapons. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30795)
Russian intelligence is recruiting Ukrainian women via Telegram to kill military personnel through dating sites; five murders are known. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30806)

sections.executive_summary: Ukraine conducted significant deep-strike operations against Russian strategic infrastructure, hitting the VNIIR-Progress defense plant in Cheboksary and the Novokuibyshevsk oil refinery in Samara at ranges exceeding 900 km. Russia continued mass drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, with Zelensky stating 650 drones and 35-100 missiles are launched daily. On the ground, Russian forces achieved notable advances into Kostiantynovka, breaking into the city center from multiple axes.

points: Ukrainian forces struck the VNIIR-Progress plant in Cheboksary, Russia, using FP-5 Flamingo missiles. The plant produces electromechanical drives, hydraulic systems, and control systems for heavy military equipment including self-propelled artillery and Iskander missile complexes. (Source: @UkraineNow/81810)

Ukraine also hit the Novokuibyshevsk oil refinery in Samara, located over 900 km from the front line. The refinery is one of the largest in the region, part of Rosneft, producing over 30 types of petroleum products with a capacity of about 7 million tons per year. (Source: @UkraineNow/81810)

A joint operation by the Azov National Guard, SBU, and special units destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure including power substations, radar equipment, a control tower, repair infrastructure, and fuel tanks. The shadow fleet vessel Lady Augusta was also destroyed, rendering the port completely de-energized. (Source: @UkraineNow/81817)

Zelensky stated Ukraine is responding to Russian attacks with 300-350 drones per day and aims to reach a level of full response capability. He made these remarks at a press conference in Tallinn. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802)
Zelensky proposed that the UK confiscate Roman Abramovich's £2.4 billion from the sale of Chelsea Football Club and use it to purchase air defense systems for Ukraine. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30786)

In Odesa's Prymorskyi district, two residential buildings were damaged by a Russian attack. One apartment on the 5th floor was completely burned, and a 46-year-old woman was hospitalized. (Source: @UkraineNow/81809)

Three officials of an army corps in Kharkiv region are suspected of extorting military personnel, demanding money for inclusion in reports for additional payments. The scheme allegedly operated since late 2025, with total illicit gains of 274,000 hryvnias. (Source: @UkraineNow/81815)

Russia launched 650 drones and 35-100 missiles per day at Ukraine according to Zelensky. Overnight, 326 Ukrainian drones were reportedly shot down over Russian regions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802) (Source: @tass_agency/380029)
A fuel container caught fire in Rostov region from falling drone debris; three people were injured in Samara region from drone attacks on industrial facilities. (Source: @tass_agency/380029)

Eight districts of Kherson region were completely de-energized due to Ukrainian drone attacks. Cheboksary was hit by a missile attack early morning on June 10, with three people injured. (Source: @tass_agency/380029)

Russian intelligence agencies are recruiting Ukrainian women via Telegram to carry out killings of military personnel through dating sites. Five murders are known and one was prevented. Handlers provide funds for rent, alcohol, and methadone at designated drop-off points. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30806)

The head of the Main Rocket and Artillery Directorate of the Russian Defense Ministry, Colonel-General Damir Davidov, was reportedly killed in a car explosion in Balashikha near Moscow. He was directly involved in planning the full-scale invasion. (Source: @UkraineNow/81805)
The Alternative for Germany party demanded Ukraine compensate Germany for Nord Stream 2 losses and explain its role in the incident. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30803)

Mercedes is reportedly planning cooperation with Ukrainian drone startup Tytan Technologies to create mobile anti-drone defense groups based on Mercedes G-class military vehicles for protecting German critical infrastructure. (Source: @tass_agency/380024)

Russian troops broke into Kostiantynovka city center from multiple areas. The most significant advances were recorded from Illinovka and Berestok, where assault groups advanced through the 7 Vetrov shopping center and Yubileiny Park to Yemelyanova Street and toward the Krivoy Torets River northwest of the railway station. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30804)

Ukrainian forces destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure, significantly complicating Russian military logistics in the area. The port is completely de-energized with destroyed power substations, radar, control towers, and fuel storage. (Source: @UkraineNow/81817)

Multiple groups of Russian UAVs were reported moving from Belgorod region toward Kharkiv, and from northern Kharkiv region on a southeastern course. KAB guided bombs were reported heading toward southeastern Kharkiv region and Donetsk region. (Source: @kpszsu/64702) (Source: @kpszsu/64724) (Source: @kpszsu/64725)

UAV groups were reported near Balakliia moving toward Zlatopil, with additional drones targeting Pechenihy from the south. Four groups of drones were tracked near Balakliia. (Source: @eRadarrua/96104)

Strike UAVs were reported in the south of Kharkiv region heading north, and multiple UAVs were reported in the north and east of Sumy region moving westward. A jet-powered UAV was reported entering Sumy region from Kursk region on a northern course. (Source: @kpszsu/64698) (Source: @kpszsu/64695) (Source: @kpszsu/64691)
UAVs were reported in Chernihiv region north of Hlukhiv, and in Sumy region near Krolevets heading toward Sumy from the north. (Source: @eRadarrua/96103)

Strike UAVs were reported in the north and east of Sumy region moving in a westward direction. A jet-powered UAV entered Sumy region from Kursk region on a northern course. (Source: @kpszsu/64695) (Source: @kpszsu/64691)

UAVs were reported between Boromlia and Trostianets in Sumy region, and between Kobylyaky and Bilyky in Poltava region on a course toward Kozelshchyna. (Source: @eRadarrua/96106) (Source: @eRadarrua/96102)
KAB guided bombs were reported heading toward southeastern Kharkiv region and Dnipropetrovsk region. (Source: @kpszsu/64720)
A group of enemy UAVs from Kherson region was reported heading toward Zaporizhzhia. UAVs were also reported in the southern part of Zaporizhzhia city. (Source: @kpszsu/64693) (Source: @kpszsu/64727)

A group of jet-powered UAVs from the Black Sea was reported heading toward Kherson region. A UAV was reported at Vylkove in Odesa region from the Black Sea. (Source: @kpszsu/64721) (Source: @kpszsu/64692)
In occupied Melitopol, a local power substation was attacked by UAVs, leaving the city without electricity. (Source: @eRadarrua/96105)

UAVs from southern Dnipropetrovsk region were reported heading toward Kryvyi Rih, with additional drones reported at Solone, Pyatyhatki, and heading toward Kryvyi Rih from the north. (Source: @kpszsu/64699) (Source: @eRadarrua/96112)

A Lightning UAV was reported passing Snihurivka toward Bereznehuvate in Mykolaiv region. UAVs were reported heading toward Slavhorod from the north in Dnipropetrovsk region. (Source: @eRadarrua/96104) (Source: @eRadarrua/96106)
Eight districts of Kherson region were completely de-energized due to Ukrainian drone attacks. (Source: @tass_agency/380029)
Russia launched 650 drones and 35-100 missiles per day at Ukraine. Ukraine responded with 300-350 drones per day and aims to reach full response capability. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802)
Overnight, 326 Ukrainian drones were reportedly shot down over Russian regions. Fires hit the Novokuibyshevsk refinery in Samara and a fuel container in Rostov region. (Source: @tass_agency/380029)

Ukraine used FP-5 Flamingo missiles to strike the VNIIR-Progress plant in Cheboksary, nearly 1,000 km from Ukraine. The attack was confirmed with reports of the plant building heavily damaged and three people injured. (Source: @eRadarrua/96114) (Source: @eRadarrua/96109) (Source: @eRadarrua/96120)
Cheboksary was hit by a missile attack early morning on June 10, with three people injured. The missile threat in Chuvashia was later cancelled but UAV threat remained. (Source: @tass_agency/380029)

Multiple waves of UAVs were tracked across numerous Ukrainian regions including Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, and Odesa regions, indicating a coordinated large-scale drone campaign. (Source: @kpszsu/64699) (Source: @kpszsu/64693) (Source: @eRadarrua/96103) (Source: @eRadarrua/96104)
KAB guided bombs were reported heading toward southeastern Kharkiv region, Donetsk region, and Dnipropetrovsk region. (Source: @kpszsu/64725) (Source: @kpszsu/64720)

key_developments: Ukraine conducted deep strikes using FP-5 Flamingo missiles against the VNIIR-Progress defense plant in Cheboksary and the Novokuibyshevsk oil refinery in Samara, over 900 km from the front line, targeting critical Russian military-industrial and energy infrastructure. (Source: @UkraineNow/81810)

Russian forces broke into Kostiantynovka city center from Illinovka and Berestok, advancing through the 7 Vetrov shopping center and Yubileiny Park to Yemelyanova Street and the Krivoy Torets River northwest of the railway station, with Ukrainian experts describing defense prospects as very bleak. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30804)

Russia launched 650 drones and 35-100 missiles per day at Ukraine; overnight 326 Ukrainian drones were shot down over Russian territory, with attacks causing fires at the Samara refinery, a fuel container in Rostov region, and power outages across eight Kherson region districts. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30802) (Source: @tass_agency/380029)

A joint operation by the Azov National Guard, SBU, and special units destroyed Mariupol port infrastructure including power substations, radar equipment, control towers, fuel tanks, and the shadow fleet vessel Lady Augusta, completely de-energizing the port and complicating Russian logistics. (Source: @UkraineNow/81817)

The EU Commission proposed a 21st sanctions package targeting Russian energy and fishing sectors, and proposed banning entry to the EU for all Russian military personnel involved in the war against Ukraine. (Source: @UkraineNow/81798)

Russian intelligence is recruiting Ukrainian women via Telegram and dating sites to assassinate military personnel, with five murders confirmed and one prevented; handlers provide funds for expenses and methadone at designated drop-off points. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30806)

Bulgaria announced it will no longer supply weapons to Ukraine, with Defense Minister Stoyanov stating Kiev needs more people, not more weapons, and calling for a just peace with terms determined by both sides. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30795)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is intensifying on multiple axes. Ukraine's deep-strike campaign against strategic Russian infrastructure at ranges exceeding 900 km demonstrates growing long-range strike capability with FP-5 Flamingo missiles, targeting defense-industrial facilities and energy infrastructure critical to Russia's war effort. Russia continues mass daily drone and missile attacks with 650 drones and 35-100 missiles per day, maintaining pressure on Ukrainian civilian and military targets. The fall of Kostiantynovka's city center to Russian assault groups represents a significant territorial development on the eastern front. Russia's use of hybrid tactics including recruiting Ukrainian women for assassinations and the reported killing of a senior Russian general near Moscow indicate escalating unconventional operations on both sides.
sections.regional_response: The EU is advancing its 21st sanctions package targeting Russian energy and fishing sectors, with a proposed ban on EU entry for all Russian military personnel involved in the war. Bulgaria has broken ranks with the Western coalition by halting all weapons supplies to Ukraine, signaling potential fractures in allied support. France is symbolically supporting Ukraine by featuring Ukrainian-painted Mirage 2000 jets flown by joint Ukrainian-French crews at the July 14 military parade. Germany faces domestic pressure as the Alternative for Germany party demands Ukrainian compensation for Nord Stream 2 losses, while Mercedes reportedly explores anti-drone cooperation with Ukrainian defense startup Tytan Technologies.
sections.intelligence_notes: The scale of drone operations is notable: Russia claims 326 Ukrainian drones were intercepted in a single night across multiple Russian regions, while Ukraine reports deploying 300-350 drones daily against Russia. The targeting of the VNIIR-Progress plant, which produces components for Iskander missiles and Shahed drones, suggests Ukraine is systematically degrading Russia's ability to produce the weapons used against it. The destruction of Mariupol port infrastructure by Azov, SBU, and special units indicates continued Ukrainian special operations capability deep behind enemy lines. The reported death of Colonel-General Damir Davidov, head of Russia's Main Rocket and Artillery Directorate, if confirmed, would represent a significant blow to Russian military leadership. Over 60,000 Ukrainian military-age men have entered Germany in 16 months, raising questions about mobilization and refugee dynamics.
